title: Nasal congestion
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped unilateral, visual, epistaxis, fever, facial-pain, purulent-discharge, headache, and worsening fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

examination.text: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped rhinitis terminology, congestion, rhinorrhoea, sneezing, postnasal drip, smell, eye, trigger, timing, and selected nasal-exam fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

investigations.text: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.
investigations.rationale: Reviewed for clinician-selected allergy testing; no test-result legacy item was specific enough to map.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis

investigations.text: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented allergy education, nasal care, medicine plan, return, and follow-up fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.

source_limitations: Sinus congestion, catarrh, detailed nasal-mucosa, throat, ear, mastoid, lymph-node, shiner, and fixed two-to-four-week fields remain unsupported.
Allergic rhinitis remains clinician-stated and the broad nasal-congestion presentation includes non-allergic causes.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
